qa_pairs.question: Why is it necessary to renew Alliance accounts annually?
qa_pairs.answer: Annual renewal of user accounts is essential to gather up-to-date information for granting agencies and to deactivate accounts that are no longer in use.

qa_pairs.question: Who is required to renew their Alliance account, and by when?
qa_pairs.answer: All Alliance users must renew their account between May 6 and June 9, 2026. Failure to do so will result in automatic deactivation, though data won't be immediately deleted, and accounts can be renewed later.

qa_pairs.question: Do I need to renew my Alliance account if I created it recently?
qa_pairs.answer: No, if you created your account after January 1, you are exempt from the current renewal period. However, you must still accept new policies and consents when you first log in to CCDB.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the Privacy Policy and other agreements I've accepted?
qa_pairs.answer: All agreements you accept on CCDB, including policies, consents, and optional transfers, can be viewed on the Agreements page at ccdb.alliancecan.ca/agreements/user_index. Policies are also on the Alliance website.

qa_pairs.question: What is a primary role, and why is it important for Alliance accounts?
qa_pairs.answer: Your primary role should be your main position (e.g., full-time faculty or the one with your primary supervisor). It helps manage accounts with multiple roles, facilitates communication, and aids in reporting. Not renewing your primary role will deactivate your account.

qa_pairs.question: Can I change my primary role in Alliance?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, users with multiple active roles can change their primary role at any time on CCDB's 'Home' page by checking the 'Make this role primary' box next to the desired role.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if my situation, like my faculty position or academic level, has changed?
qa_pairs.answer: If your role has changed, you must apply for a new role through CCDB under 'My Account' → 'Apply for a new role'. You can also disable old roles if they are no longer needed.

qa_pairs.question: My Alliance account has expired and been deactivated, but I still need to access my data to move it. What should I do?
qa_pairs.answer: Please send an email to support@tech.alliancecan.ca for assistance with accessing your data.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact if my account renewal question isn't answered in the FAQ?
qa_pairs.answer: For further assistance with account renewal questions, please send an email to renewals@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if a faculty member (PI) does not renew their Alliance account by the deadline?
qa_pairs.answer: If a faculty member allows their account to expire, their account and the accounts of all sponsored users will be deactivated. Any jobs submitted by them or their sponsored users will be paused, and their research group will lose file access until the sponsor account is reactivated.

qa_pairs.question: As a faculty member, what steps are involved in renewing my Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: You'll receive an email with a link to the CCDB renewal form. You need to update your contact info, Field of Research, complete a User Satisfaction Survey, and update the status of your sponsored roles. Finally, click the 'REQUEST ACCOUNT RENEWAL' button.

qa_pairs.question: If I'm a Principal Investigator moving to a different institution, will users sponsored by my old role automatically transfer to my new role?
qa_pairs.answer: No, users you wish to sponsor with your new faculty role will need to apply for a new role sponsored by your new position. If you have a RAC allocation, sponsored users with old roles will lose access to the associated Resource Allocation Project (RAP).

qa_pairs.question: Can I access files from a sponsored user I've indicated has moved on and is no longer under my sponsorship?
qa_pairs.answer: Consent from that user may be required to access their files. It's important to make arrangements with the sponsored user as soon as possible to mitigate risks if they become incommunicado.

qa_pairs.question: As a sponsored user (e.g., Doctoral, Masters, External Collaborator), how do I renew my account?
qa_pairs.answer: You'll receive an email directing you to the CCDB renewal form. Update your contact information, fill out the form and a User Satisfaction Survey, then click the 'REQUEST ACCOUNT RENEWAL' button.

qa_pairs.question: My sponsored role is still deactivated even after I completed my account renewal on CCDB. What's wrong?
qa_pairs.answer: Your sponsored role remains deactivated because your sponsor has not yet renewed or reactivated their own account and confirmed your role. It should activate once your sponsor completes their renewal.

qa_pairs.question: I renewed my Alliance account with the wrong sponsored role. How can I correct this?
qa_pairs.answer: You should apply for a new role reflecting your current information through CCDB under 'My Account' → 'Apply for a new role'. Your old role will automatically expire if not renewed, and your sponsor will confirm the new one.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I'm trying to reactivate an old sponsored role but my sponsor cannot confirm it?
qa_pairs.answer: Please send an email to renewals@tech.alliancecan.ca for assistance with this request.
